meta.title: Tiempos narrativos
meta.goal: Ya combinas past simple, past continuous y past perfect para contar historias con fluidez temporal.

cards.title: Cada tiempo tiene un papel
cards.lede: Una buena narración en inglés utiliza tres tiempos con funciones distintas y complementarias. Entenderlos como capas te ayuda a usarlos juntos con naturalidad.

rows.value: Los eventos principales, en orden cronológico: She arrived, opened the door, and saw him.
El fondo o escenario, y acciones interrumpidas: It was raining. She was reading when the phone rang.
Lo que ocurrió antes del momento central de la historia: He had already left when I called.

tip.label: Imagen mental:
tip.body: el past simple es el primer plano; el past continuous, el fondo; el past perfect, el flashback.

cards.title: Past continuous para crear atmósfera
cards.lede: Al inicio de una narración, el past continuous pinta el escenario antes de que comience la acción principal.

columns.label: Past simple (seco, sin contexto)
columns.rule: Solo hechos, sin atmósfera ni inmersión

columns.label: Past continuous (inmersivo)
columns.rule: Crea el ambiente antes de que entre la acción principal
examples: I was walking to the park when I saw him.
She was waiting at the station when the train arrived.
We were sitting in the café when the argument started.

tip.label: Estructura clásica:
tip.body: Past continuous (escenario) + when + past simple (evento): I was cooking when the fire alarm went off.

cards.title: Past perfect para dar contexto
cards.lede: El past perfect explica por qué las cosas pasaron de cierta manera. Sin él, el lector/oyente no entiende la causa o la lógica de los eventos.

lines.text: She cried when he left because she loved him for years.
lines.note: sin past perfect, no queda claro el tiempo de cada acción

lines.text: She cried when he left because she had loved him for years.
lines.note: had loved clarifica que ese amor precedía a la despedida

lines.text: When I arrived, the meeting started and everyone went home.
lines.note: parece que todo pasó a la vez, en orden incorrecto

lines.text: When I arrived, the meeting had already started and half the people had gone home.
lines.note: had started / had gone = antes de mi llegada

cards.title: Los tres tiempos en un párrafo
cards.lede: Este párrafo muestra cómo los tres tiempos trabajan juntos. Observa qué aporta cada uno.

rows.value: It was a cold Tuesday morning and I was rushing to catch my train.
I had forgotten to set my alarm the night before.
I ran out of the house, hailed a cab, and arrived just in time.
The other passengers were already boarding when I got there.

cards.title: Las palabras que señalan cada tiempo
cards.lede: Los conectores temporales son pistas sobre qué tiempo verbal usar. Aprenderlos te ayuda a tomar decisiones rápidas mientras hablas.

rows.value: → sugieren continuidad o coincidencia: while she was sleeping / as I was leaving

rows.key: after / once / by the time / already
rows.value: → señalan anterioridad: after she had left / I had already eaten by the time you called
→ indican secuencia de eventos puntuales: then she turned around / suddenly he appeared

cards.title: Errores comunes al narrar
cards.lede: Estos errores aparecen constantemente en nivel B2. Identificarlos y corregirlos marca la diferencia entre un relato confuso y uno fluido.

lines.text: When I have arrived, the party already finished.
lines.note: presente perfecto en lugar de past perfect

lines.text: When I arrived, the party had already finished.
She was cooking dinner and suddenly she dropped the pan.
lines.note: correcto, pero sin past perfect para explicar por qué

lines.text: She was cooking dinner and suddenly she dropped the pan. She had been distracted by the phone.
I was seeing him yesterday at the shop.
lines.note: verbos de estado no se usan en continuo

lines.text: I saw him yesterday at the shop.
lines.note: see = verbo de percepción, solo en past simple

quiz.q: ¿Cuál de estas frases usa los tiempos verbales correctamente para narrar?
options: When she arrived, the film started already.
When she arrived, the film had already started.
When she was arriving, the film already started.
When she has arrived, the film had already started.
quiz.explain: Past simple (arrived) para el evento principal + past perfect (had already started) para señalar que la película empezó ANTES de que ella llegara. La opción A carece del auxiliar 'had'; la C usa past continuous incorrectamente; la D mezcla presente perfecto con pasado.

quiz.q: Estás narrando y quieres describir el ambiente de fondo. ¿Cuál de estas opciones es la mejor apertura?
options: It rained and I walked through the empty streets.
It was raining and I was walking through the empty streets.
It had rained and I had walked through the empty streets.
It rains and I walk through the empty streets.
quiz.explain: El past continuous (was raining, was walking) se usa para establecer el escenario y la atmósfera al inicio de una narración. El past simple describe hechos concretos, no el fondo; el past perfect indicaría anterioridad.

quiz.q: Elige el conector que normalmente pide past perfect:
quiz.explain: 'By the time' señala anterioridad: by the time I arrived, they had left. 'Suddenly' y 'then' suelen ir con past simple (eventos puntuales); 'while' sugiere simultaneidad y normalmente pide past continuous.
